narrative: Dear [Student's Name], imagine the admissions team looking at your application as a story of your journey toward becoming a lawyer, piecing together the chapters from your undergrad days through law school to predict if you'd conquer the bar exam. Your story starts strong in their eyes with your undergraduate GPA of 2.9 and LSAT score of 36, but it was your performance in law school that truly set the tone—landing in the 2nd decile for both Year 1 and Year 3 grades stood out as the biggest signal, whispering that the rigors of legal studies might prove an uphill battle ahead. These early and later rankings were the strongest forces pulling the prediction toward a 45.4% chance of not passing the bar, painting a picture of consistent challenges in keeping pace with peers. Your middle family income added another layer, suggesting fewer resources to buffer those hurdles compared to others. Then there were the quieter influences: your undergraduate GPA, your background as a white male pursuing full-time studies, all weaving in to reinforce that narrative of risk. It's not that any one piece defined you, but together they formed a cautious tale the model couldn't ignore, leading to the tough call on your application. The good news? This is just one snapshot—your determination could rewrite the story with targeted improvements in study strategies or extra support. You've got the drive; now it's about aligning the details to shift that prediction. We're rooting for you to prove it wrong and shine on the bar. Keep pushing forward.
